revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jingūjiyama Kofun]][[ja:神宮寺山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kita-ku, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Jingūjiyama Kofun | native_name =神宮寺山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Jingu-ji-yama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Jingūjiyama Kofun, Anterior to the right; Posterior to the left | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Jingūjiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.683739|N|133.930492|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|en|Kita-ku, Okayama|qid=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku, Okayama|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid 4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|41|1.46|N|133|55|49.77|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Jingūjiyama Kofun'''|神宮寺山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nakai neighborhood of {{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|en|Kita-ku, Okayama|qid=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku, Okayama|1=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1959.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2283|title=神宮寺山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Jingūjiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole when viewed from above. It is located on the {{ill|Alluvial plain|en|Alluvial plain|qid=Q656586|lt=Alluvial plain|1=Alluvial plain}} on the west bank of the {{ill|Asahi River|en|Asahi River|qid=Q2866291|lt=Asahi River|1=Asahi River}}, in the south of Okayama Prefecture, slightly north of central Okayama city. The tumulus is currently occupied by the {{nihongo|Amahakari Shrine|天計神社||}}, an ancient {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} listed in the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} ''[[Engishiki]]'' records; however, its appears that this shrine was relocated to this site from its original location in the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} by order of {{ill|Kobayakawa Hideaki|en|Kobayakawa Hideaki|qid=Q1369568|lt=Kobayakawa Hideaki|1=Kobayakawa Hideaki}}. The tumulus was also located in the grounds of a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|qid=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temple|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}}, the Myōhōzan Jingū-ji, from which it takes its name, but this temple was closed in the {{ill|Kanbun|en|Kanbun|qid=Q7901319|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun}} era (1661-1672) of the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. However, during the period that it was within the grounds of the temple, the anterior portion of the tumulus was turned into a cemetery, and the posterior circular portion was flattened. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is orientated to the west, has a total length of about 155 meters, and a round posterior portion with a diameter of about 70 meters and a height of about 13 meters and was built in three stages. The anterior part has a length of 75 meters, height of seven meters and was built in two stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' and shards of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found, but is it uncertain of the tumulus originally had a moat. The main [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a vertical pit-style stone chamber located at the top of the posterior circular portion, and there also a pit-type auxiliary chamber for the burial of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} nearby. The cover stone for the burial chamber is exposed under the floor of the [[Haiden (Shinto)|''Haiden'']] of the Amahakari Shrine on the top the mound, but as it has yet to be [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], the details of the interior are unknown. The auxiliary chamber, which measures1.50 meters in length, 0.50-0.60 meters in width, and about 0.90 meters in height was excavated in 1961, and more than 100 iron weapons (swords, spears) and iron agricultural tools (scythes, axes, chisels, drills, and saws) were found. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
It is speculated that there may be a secondary burial chamber in the anterior portion of the tumulus which has yet to be discovered as fragments of swords, armor, and spears have been unearthed in that area the past.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the latter half of the early to early middleKofun period, around the latter half of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century, based on the unearthed ''haniwa''. Most of the tumuli in the Kibi region are built on hilly terrain, so this tumulus attracted attention because it was built on alluvial land, which is normally considered unsuitable for large Kofuns. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Amahakari-jinja, haiden.jpg|Amahakari-jinja, Haiden Jingu-ji-yama Kofun, kouenbu-1-2.jpg|View of the rear circle from the front Jingu-ji-yama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|Looking forward from the rear circular part </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commonscat|Jingu-ji-yama Kofun}} *[https://www.city.okayama.jp/museum/kofun1/03.html Okayama City home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/uploaded/attachment/260844.pdf Okayama Prefecture home page] {{Kofun navbar}}
[[en:Jingūjiyama Kofun]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Jingu-ji-yama Kofun}} [[ja:神宮寺山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q28657616}} [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
